test_name: Gemma 4 E2B vLLM + CPU Offloading + BNB_INT4 + TurboQuant KV

steps.name: find_model_profile
steps.details: Found gemma-4-e2b-it-vllm: Gemma 4 E2B Edge Instruct (vLLM BF16) (2.3B, BF16)

steps.name: size_estimates
steps.details: BF16=11710 MB, INT4=4145 MB, VRAM=4096 MB, RAM=24079 MB

steps.name: select_quantization_without_offload
steps.details: select_quantization() returned: None (expected None — too large for VRAM alone)

warnings: Model exceeds VRAM (INT4=4145 MB > 2536 MB available). CPU offloading 2.1 GB to system RAM (24079 MB available).
BF16 model (11710 MB) will be quantized to bnb_int4 (~4145 MB) via bitsandbytes
enforce_eager=True (saves ~1 GB VRAM on small GPUs)
Multimodal limited to 1 image, no audio (low VRAM)

steps.name: load_model_with_cpu_offload
steps.details:
Load failed: 1 validation error for ModelConfig
  Value error, The checkpoint you are trying to load has model type `gemma4` but Transformers does not recognize this architecture. This could be because of an issue with the checkpoint, or because your version of Transformers is out of date.

You can update Transformers with the command `pip install --upgrade transformers`. If this does not work, and the checkpoint is very new, then there may not be a release version that supports this model yet. In this case, you can get the most up-to-date code by installing Transformers from source with the command `pip install git+https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.git` [type=value_error, input_value=ArgsKwargs((), {'model': ...rocessor_plugin': None}), input_type=ArgsKwargs]
